Home/International Removals/the Canary Islands
The Move

The Canary Islands are Spanish soil, and we ship there year round.

Thousands of British movers have made the Canaries home, and we run a dependable service to Tenerife, Gran Canaria and the smaller islands alike. Your container travels via mainland Spain before its final sea crossing.

Sea freight suits a full household, and air freight covers anything you would rather have sooner.

Popular destinations we ship to
TenerifeGran CanariaLanzaroteFuerteventuraLa Palma
Typical Transit Times
Sole-use container2–3 weeks
Shared (groupage) container3–4 weeks
Air freight1–2 weeks
Times shown are to port. Please allow additional time for onward island delivery. We recommend booking 6 to 8 weeks ahead of your move date.
Your options

Choose the right way to ship

Every move to the Canary Islands is different, so we shape the service around yours.

Sea · Sole-use

Your own 20ft or 40ft container

Your belongings travel alone, packed and sealed at your door and not opened again until they reach you. The fastest and most secure sea option.

Best for: full household moves
Sea · Shared

Groupage / part-load container

Your goods share container space with other shipments on the same route, splitting the cost between several moves. Slightly longer transit, but excellent value.

Best for: smaller moves, flexible budgets
Air Freight

Fast-track for essentials

Air freight suits the things you need soon after you land, such as documents, clothing and everyday items. It arrives in a fraction of the time.

Best for: essentials & smaller shipments
Excess Baggage

A few boxes or suitcases

Not moving a whole household? Our excess baggage service is a simple, affordable way to send personal items and smaller consignments overseas.

Best for: light movers & students
Good to know

The Canary Islands sit outside standard Spanish VAT, and that works in your favour

The islands operate their own local tax, IGIC, separate from mainland Spanish VAT. Combined with the usual relief on used household goods, most movers find the paperwork more straightforward than they expect.

IGIC, not mainland VAT

The Canaries use their own local tax system, separate from standard Spanish VAT.

Your NIE number

Most imports need your NIE, the Spanish identification number for foreigners.

Duty relief on household goods

Used personal effects usually enter free of duty when you transfer your main residence.

Residency evidence

Proof of your move supports the relief claim on your shipment.
